steps.title: Use the zero product property
steps.content:
The equation is already factored:
$$
(x-16)(x-12)(x+7)(x+13)=0
$$
A product equals $0$ when at least one factor equals $0$.

steps.title: Find the positive roots
steps.content: Set the factors equal to $0$. The equations $x-16=0$, $x-12=0$, $x+7=0$, and $x+13=0$ give the roots $16$, $12$, $-7$, and $-13$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: A graphing tool can also show the $x$-intercepts of $$ (x-16)(x-12)(x+7)(x+13)=0 $$ The comma separates the two roots; it is not the number $1612$. The answer is 16,12.
